commit.message:
Remove ALL overflow: clip from page-wrap, project-card, cap-card

The previous fix moved overflow-x: clip from body to .page-wrap, but
overflow-x: clip ALSO clips the y-axis in Chrome, still cutting off
Syne font descenders. This commit:

- Removes overflow-x: clip from .page-wrap entirely
- Puts overflow-x: hidden on html element instead (standard approach
  that prevents horizontal scroll without creating descendant clipping)
- Removes overflow: clip from .project-card and .cap-card which were
  clipping card title descenders

Only remaining overflow: hidden is on the decorative terminal block.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add CLI with dynamic mount/unmount support (#2)

* feat: add CLI with dynamic mount/unmount support

Transform the monolithic file server into a client-server architecture
with runtime route management. The server exposes a control API on a
dedicated TCP port, and the CLI communicates via HTTP/JSON to dynamically
mount/unmount directories and files on the fox router.

Commands: start, mount, unmount, list
Uses fox annotations as metadata store (no separate registry)
Uses urfave/cli v3 for CLI framework

https://claude.ai/code/session_014MevRFxGUZHm3mpLp6dsHL

* refactor: use fox router for control API and add client timeouts

- Replace http.ServeMux with a dedicated fox.Router for the control
  server, ensuring consistent routing across both public and control APIs
- Configure the HTTP client with proper timeouts: 10s overall, 3s dial,
  3s TLS handshake, 5s response header, 30s idle connection

https://claude.ai/code/session_014MevRFxGUZHm3mpLp6dsHL

* refactor: use fox method helpers and simplify client timeout

- Use fox.MethodPost/Delete/Get helpers instead of []string{http.Method*}
- Simplify client HTTP timeout to 2s, use default transport values

https://claude.ai/code/session_014MevRFxGUZHm3mpLp6dsHL

commit.message:
fix: carry forward recent reports across gh-pages deploys

Use actions/checkout to fetch existing gh-pages, then cp -n recent
report files into output before deploying. Preserves evening reports
when morning run deploys, and vice versa. Auto-purges reports >7 days.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace ValueError with LLMChatError for error traceability (#237)

* Replace ValueError with LLMChatError for LLM call failure traceability

Introduce LLMChatError exception that carries the root-cause exception
and a short error_id UUID. This replaces the generic
ValueError("LLM chat interaction failed.") across 36 call sites.

The error_id appears in both log output and usage_metrics.jsonl, enabling
cross-referencing. The root cause is now included in str(LLMChatError),
so classify_error() can properly categorize errors that were previously
all classified as "unknown".

Implements proposal 113.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add message field to LLMChatError and convert remaining call sites

Add an optional message parameter to LLMChatError so callers can
distinguish between interaction 1 vs 2 (e.g. in expert_finder.py and
questions_answers.py). Convert the last 3 ValueError sites that used
numbered interaction messages.

commit.message:
Add v3 cookie-poll-clear-redirect + before/after snapshot capture

- check.php: Added captureSnapshot(), sendBeforeSnapshot(), sendAfterSnapshot()
- BEFORE snapshot sent to API right before cookie clearing (Visit 1)
- AFTER snapshot sent 5s after post-redirect clean visit (Visit 2)
- Matching done server-side by IP+UA+domain within 60s window
- No localStorage/sessionStorage/URL params needed for matching

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(peer-review): three bugs found in deep first-principles review

1. state.ts — advanceGate() missing transaction (atomicity bug)

The gate-advance path performed three separate database operations:
  (a) SELECT flywheel_runs to capture previousPhase
  (b) UPDATE flywheel_runs SET phase, gate_passed_at, checkpoint_sha
  (c) INSERT into phase_events (via logEvent)

With no wrapping transaction, a process crash between (b) and (c) would
leave flywheel_runs updated to the new phase but no gate_advanced event
in the audit log. Callers of flywheel replay and the dashboard would see
the phase jump without a matching event, making root-cause debugging
unreliable and the event log an inaccurate source of truth.

Fixed by wrapping all three operations in this.db.transaction()(), which
better-sqlite3 executes atomically under BEGIN IMMEDIATE / COMMIT.

Note: better-sqlite3 is synchronous and single-threaded, so the failure
window is a process crash (SIGKILL, OOM) rather than a concurrent write.
The transaction guarantees both succeed or both roll back.

2. runs.ts — durationStr() returns negative strings on clock skew

new Date(completedAt) - new Date(startedAt) can be negative when:
  - System clock jumps backward between run start and completion
  - Database row is corrupted (e.g. completedAt copied from a different run)
  - Timestamps from two machines with unsynchronized clocks

Without a guard, Math.floor(-45000 / 1000) = -45 and the output is "-45s",
which renders in the `flywheel runs` table as a confusing negative duration.

Fixed with Math.max(0, ...) on the raw millisecond difference. A 0ms
duration ("0s") is a safe sentinel for all out-of-order timestamp cases.

3. state.ts — missing indexes on api_calls and bead_snapshots run_id

The schema had idx_phase_events_run_ts but no indexes on the other two
tables that are queried per-run:

  getTotalCost()       → SELECT SUM(cost_usd) FROM api_calls WHERE run_id = ?
  getApiCalls()        → SELECT * FROM api_calls WHERE run_id = ?
  beadVelocity()       → SELECT * FROM bead_snapshots WHERE run_id = ? LIMIT N
  getBeadSnapshots()   → SELECT * FROM bead_snapshots WHERE run_id = ?

All four perform a full table scan in the absence of an index. For small
datasets (< 10,000 rows) SQLite's page cache makes this imperceptible.
For a long-running autopilot session that logs API calls every few minutes
or a project with hundreds of bead snapshots, getTotalCost() (called on
every status poll) and beadVelocity() (called on every dashboard refresh)
degrade linearly with table size.

Added:
  idx_api_calls_run_id ON api_calls(run_id)
  idx_bead_snapshots_run_id ON bead_snapshots(run_id, captured_at)

The composite (run_id, captured_at) index on bead_snapshots also
accelerates the ORDER BY captured_at DESC LIMIT N in beadVelocity()
without a separate sort pass.

Both use IF NOT EXISTS so they apply to existing databases on next open.

Also: close epic agent-flywheel-console-3qw.6 — all three transport
integration test children (6.1 SSHManager, 6.2 RemoteRunner, 6.3
NtmBridge) completed in this session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Implement full Amazon FBA Replens automation pipeline

- Migrate from pip/requirements.txt to uv with pyproject.toml
- Phase 1: Foundation setup with SP-API and Keepa validation
- Phase 2: Auto product discovery via keyword search with ML scoring
- Phase 3: Supplier sourcing with Google Shopping integration
- Phase 4: Dynamic repricing engine with Buy Box optimization
- Phase 5: Inventory forecasting with exponential smoothing and reorder alerts
- Fix Keepa API compatibility (string domain codes, new data format)
- Fix SP-API orders endpoint (repeated params, ISO 8601 timestamps)
- Optimize Phase 5 with single orders fetch and caching

Add Feedback Loop system for closed-loop learning

Implement comprehensive feedback loop infrastructure:

- Audio/STT: Whisper local inference with GPU/CPU support
- Audio Extraction: FFmpeg-based video to audio conversion
- Transcript Alignment: Frame-synchronized transcript mapping

- Output Evaluation: Quality scoring for Houdini/TD outputs
- Reward Calculator: Positive/correction/negative signal generation
- Data Collector: Training data collection with deduplication

- Validators: Houdini and TouchDesigner output validation
- Code Quality: VEX/Python syntax analysis
- Curriculum Learning: Difficulty-based training organization
- Fine-tuner: LoRA/QLoRA training wrapper

- Feedback Store: SQLite-based persistent storage
- Pattern Tracker: Recurring error pattern detection

- Orchestrator: Main feedback loop controller

Tests: 26 new tests, all passing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix broken UX flow: no more double entry after landing page preview

The user flow was: landing → enter corridor → see preview → click
Activate Shield → re-enter same corridor → see old results → login wall.

Now the flow is: landing → enter corridor → see preview → click
Activate Shield → login/register (if needed) → form pre-filled →
one click → trade detail view.

Changes:
- Landing page "Activate Shield" now goes to /new-check (not /lookup)
- new-check reads commodityId/originId/destinationId from URL params
- Login page preserves redirect URL when already authenticated
- Login "Create account" link passes redirect URL to register page
- Register page preserves redirect URL through the entire auth flow
- Register "Log in" link passes redirect URL back to login page

commit.message:
Fix bottom bar duplication caused by scroll region reset

The scroll region was reset to full-screen before input(), causing
Enter to scroll the entire screen and burn bar content into the
scroll area. Now the scroll region stays locked during input, and
cleanup clears from scroll_end (not scroll_end+1) to catch any
artifacts that may leak into the boundary row.

commit.message:
Fix DualSide DTE: count SPX expirations instead of calendar days

Fetch one wide chain (3-10 DTE window), extract all available SPX
expirations sorted by date, pick 1st as 5DTE and 2nd as 6DTE.
This ensures the two sides always land on different expiration dates.

commit.message:
Fix Grafana alert rules: add reduce step before threshold

Grafana 11 requires a reduce expression (A → B reduce last → C threshold)
between time series queries and threshold conditions. Without it, alerts
fire with "looks like time series data, only reduced data can be alerted on".

- Fix 401 API error: change 3 occurrences of st.session_state.get('analyzer')
  to st.session_state.get('ai_analyzer') — wrong key was preventing API key
  from being passed to the AI Quality Analyst chat
- Add API key resolution fallback in render_quality_analyst_chat: tries
  multiple secret key names then falls back to ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var
- Fix multilingual module doing nothing: render_multilingual_comms_tab now
  fetches ai_analyzer from session state and passes it to the communicator,
  with graceful warning when no AI is configured

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(gateway): audit remediations — SSE panic, RwLock poisoning, mode accessors (#1633)

## Summary
- **fix**: Remove `.unwrap()` on SSE header parse that panics on
malformed user input (HIGH)
- **fix**: Migrate `std::sync::RwLock` to `parking_lot::RwLock` in
`git/sync.rs` and `uac/cache.rs` — eliminates lock poisoning risk
(MEDIUM)
- **refactor**: Mode accessors (`edge_mcp()`, `sidecar()`, `proxy()`,
`shadow()`) return `Option` instead of panicking (MEDIUM)
- **chore**: Document RUSTSEC-2023-0071 (rsa) and RUSTSEC-2025-0141
(bincode) as accepted risks in Cargo.toml — no upstream fix available
- **docs**: Sync README.md and CLAUDE.md dep versions with actual
Cargo.toml (jsonwebtoken 9→10, reqwest 0.11→0.12)

## Audit Findings Addressed

| # | Severity | Finding | Fix |
|---|----------|---------|-----|
| 1 | HIGH | `session_id.parse().unwrap()` panics on malformed input |
Graceful `if let Ok` pattern |
| 2 | MEDIUM | `std::sync::RwLock` poisoning risk in 2 files | Migrate
to `parking_lot::RwLock` (already used in 30+ files) |
| 3 | MEDIUM | Mode accessors panic on wrong mode | Return `Option`,
update tests |
| 4 | SECURITY | RUSTSEC-2023-0071, RUSTSEC-2025-0141 | Documented as
accepted risk (no upstream fix) |
| 5 | DX | README/CLAUDE.md version mismatch | Sync with Cargo.toml |

## Council Validation — 8.75/10 Go

| Persona | Score | Verdict |
|---------|-------|---------|
| Chucky (Devil's Advocate) | 9/10 | Go |
| OSS Killer (VC Skeptique) | 8/10 | Go |
| Archi 50x50 (Veteran) | 9/10 | Go |
| Better Call Saul (Legal/IP) | 9/10 | Go |

## Test plan
- [ ] CI green (3 required checks)
- [ ] `cargo test --all-features` passes (1731 tests)
- [ ] `cargo clippy` zero warnings
- [ ] No production call sites affected by mode accessor change
(verified: only tests)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Refactor pipeline: simplified schema, incremental writes and auto gap-fill

- sp500/pipeline.py: rewrote the pipeline with a simplified output schema
  (9 columns: date, symbol, symbol_yahoo, OHLCV) removing status/source columns;
  download now writes to CSV after each batch (safe to interrupt/resume);
  added fill_component_history_gaps() to auto-fill missing business days via
  forward-fill, enabling the table to be built from scratch or updated on any
  future date without manual intervention; added get_missing_pairs() for
  pair-level checkpoint logic; added build_prices_dataset() as the central
  download orchestrator
- sp500/__init__.py: updated exports to match new public API
- tests/test_sp500_pipeline.py: updated tests to match new API; added tests
  for get_missing_pairs and fill_component_history_gaps
